title: Search Template Page System — Answer Pages, Not Blogs

structure.h1: Exact query-style title matching search intent (e.g., '$20,000 Loan at 8% for 5 Years: Monthly Payment Breakdown')
above_fold: Direct answer (the number the user searched for)
Input assumptions summary (loan amount, rate, term used)
CTA button: 'Adjust Numbers' linking to parent calculator

body_sections.how_we_calculated_this: Formula explained in plain language. Show the actual math with the user's numbers plugged in.
body_sections.monthly_payment_table: Amortization or payment schedule table with key numbers
body_sections.comparison_context: How this compares to common scenarios (if relevant)
body_sections.common_mistakes: Warnings about misunderstanding the calculation (e.g., 'This doesn't include taxes and insurance')
body_sections.faq: 3-5 questions based on query type, with concise answers
body_sections.try_it_yourself: Embed or link to parent calculator with instructions

internal_linking.page_to_parent: Strong contextual link to parent money page calculator
internal_linking.parent_to_children: Parent money page links to selected high-value scenarios only
internal_linking.rule: Scenarios should not become stronger than parent money page

example_page_loan_20000_8pct_5yr.h1: $20,000 Loan at 8% for 5 Years: Monthly Payment Breakdown
example_page_loan_20000_8pct_5yr.worked_example: Step 1: Convert 8% to monthly rate → 0.00667. Step 2: Calculate (1+r)^60 = 1.4898. Step 3: Numerator = 20000 × 0.00667 × 1.4898 = 198.77. Step 4: Denominator = 1.4898 - 1 = 0.4898. Step 5: Payment = 198.77 / 0.4898 = $405.53.
example_page_loan_20000_8pct_5yr.what_this_means: If you borrow $20,000 at 8% APR for 5 years, you'll pay $405.53 each month. Over the life of the loan, you'll pay $4,331.80 in interest — about 22% of the original loan amount.
example_page_loan_20000_8pct_5yr.common_mistakes: This assumes a fixed rate for the entire term. Variable-rate loans will change. This also doesn't include origination fees, prepayment penalties, or late fees.

faq.q: Is 8% a good rate for a $20,000 personal loan?
faq.a: As of 2026, personal loan rates range from 6% to 36%. 8% is excellent for borrowers with good to excellent credit (670+). The average rate is around 12%.

faq.q: Can I pay off a $20,000 loan early?
faq.a: Most personal loans allow early repayment without penalty, but check your loan agreement. Paying extra each month can save hundreds in interest.

faq.q: What credit score do I need for an 8% loan?
faq.a: Generally 670-720+ for rates around 8%. Below 670, expect rates of 12-20% or higher.

example_page_loan_20000_8pct_5yr.parent_link: Use our [Loan Calculator](/calculators/loan-calculator) to try different amounts, rates, and terms.

anti_patterns: DO NOT make these blog posts — they are calculator answer pages
DO NOT stuff keywords — the number IS the keyword
DO NOT add fluff paragraphs before the answer — give the number first
DO NOT make the calculator hard to find — embed or prominently link it
DO NOT use generic titles like 'Loan Payment Guide' — use the exact query
DO NOT copy-paste explanations — each page has unique numbers so explanations should reference them
